description_long: This object is a weathered, gray stone obelisk with a rectangular base and a slightly tapered, upright shaft. The surface is marred by visible cracks and chips, giving it an aged and worn appearance. The front and back faces are flat and bordered by a shallow, beveled frame, with the top corners cut at an angle to form a subtle arch. The base consists of two stacked rectangular platforms, each with sharp, defined edges and a rough stone texture. The entire structure is monolithic, with no visible joints or separations, and the stone appears to be solid throughout. There are no visible inscriptions, engravings, or decorative motifs on any side.
description: A weathered gray stone obelisk with a rectangular, stepped base and a cracked, arched top. The surface is rough and aged, with no visible inscriptions or ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the obelisk and the layered rectangular base, with visible cracks and chips along the edge.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the flat, framed face with an arched top and prominent surface cracks, set on a stepped base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the obelisk's depth, rough stone texture, and additional chipping at the base.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a similar arched, framed face as the front, with more pronounced cracks and weathering.
attribute_base_design: The base is composed of two stacked rectangular stone platforms, each with sharp, defined edges and a rough, weathered texture. The lower platform is wider, providing stability, while the upper platform is slightly smaller and supports the main shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat with the top corners cut at an angle, forming a subtle arch. There are no decorative features or unique elements at the tip.
